title: Fish and Sweet Potato Pie
excerpt: Ingredients 1.2 kg sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into smaller pieces 65 g grated ginger 800 ml milk 500 g smoked haddock, skinned and cut into 3 cm pieces 500 g stockfish fillets, skinned and cut into 3 cm pieces 400 g whole calamari, cut into 2 cm cubes or rings 400 …

seo.title: Fish and Sweet Potato Pie - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

  • 1.2 kg sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into smaller pieces
  • 65 g grated ginger
  • 800 ml milk
  • 500 g smoked haddock, skinned and cut into 3 cm pieces
  • 500 g stockfish fillets, skinned and cut into 3 cm pieces
  • 400 g whole calamari, cut into 2 cm cubes or rings
  • 400 g shelled, cleaned mussels
  • 5 – 10 ml grated garlic
  • 1 x 68 g packet white onion soup powder
  • 65 ml wholegrain mustard
  • 65 ml roughly chopped Italian parsley
  • 125 ml cream
  • 65 ml finely sliced spring onions
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • melted butter to taste
  • grated lemon zest to taste
  • extra finely chopped parsley to taste

Method:

Cook the sweet potatoes together with the ginger in a little water until soft. Drain thoroughly and keep warm.

Meanwhile heat the milk and add the haddock and stockfish. Bring to the boil and cook for 2 minutes.

Add the calamari and mussels. Cook for another minute or so and then remove the pan from the heat. Cover the pan and set aside so the fish can continue steaming through in the warm milk.

Now drain the fish well but keep the milk and pour it back into the pan. Cover the fish again and set aside.

Add the garlic to the milk and bring to the boil.

Mix the soup powder with a little cold water until smooth and whisk it into the milk with a balloon whisk.

Keep whisking until the milk has thickened.

Remove from the heat and stir in the mustard, 65 ml parsley and cream.

Pour the mixture over the fish and mix lightly.

Spoon the mixture into a deep, ovenproof dish lightly greased with butter.

Mash the sweet potatoes – but not too smooth – and stir in the spring onions.

Season to taste with salt and pepper. Spoon the mixture on top of the fish and make small notches or holes in the sweet potato.

Drizzle with the melted butter and bake in the oven at 190°C for 20 minutes or until the fish sauce is nicely bubbling up along the sides of the dish.

Set the oven to grill and grill the top of the pie until the sweet potato is golden brown and crispy here and there.

Remove from the oven and garnish with the lemon zest and extra parsley.

Serves 6 – 8 with a nice green salad.

Easy!
